decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared hospital outbreak rates per patient discharges and patient-days against regional public-hospital rates as performance benchmarks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of hospital-outbreak performance benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated hospital antibiotic-use densities and explicitly proposed surveillance with feedback and benchmarking to improve prescribing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied antibiotic-utilization surveillance and inter-hospital comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used surveillance-derived hepatitis C cascade estimates as a baseline for benchmarking elimination progress.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete use of cascade-of-care measures as surveillance benchmarks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tracked sepsis-bundle compliance against percentile benchmarks before and after implementing sepsis huddles.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied performance benchmarking to sepsis-care compliance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarked claims-based HIV quality and care-process measures across payer groups and measurement periods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete benchmarking of HIV care performance measures.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated standardized antibiotic-consumption indicators for Iran and benchmarked them against OECD-country consumption.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Applied cross-country antibiotic-utilization benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study evaluates respirator fit and uses “benchmarking” only within the BEARDS study-name expansion; it does not perform healthcare surveillance or performance benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage lead for the assigned benchmarking concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated risk-adjusted hospital measures and diagnostic-efficiency ratios to establish postoperative-imaging performance benchmarks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicenter application of hospital performance benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Audited diabetic-foot infection care against guideline recommendations used explicitly as a benchmark standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete clinical-practice benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A neonatal network used a computerized internal benchmarking program to compare antibiotic prescribing and performance across centers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multi-institution antibiotic-stewardship benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluated the accuracy and feasibility of mandatory electronic indication registration intended to support systematic antimicrobial-use benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct feasibility and data-accuracy evaluation of a benchmarking-enabling approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducted national point-prevalence surveys to compare antimicrobial use across Canadian pediatric hospitals and establish improvement goals.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicenter antimicrobial-use benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The simulated-patient study measures inappropriate antibiotic dispensing but does not describe surveillance benchmarking or comparison against performance benchmarks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit lineage contribution for healthcare benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Developed and deployed a secure web application that records hand-hygiene adherence and supplies immediate feedback with anonymized benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit software implementation of routine observation and benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Established procedure-level surgical-site infection performance benchmarks and developed a burden-based prioritization framework.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of benchmarks and a new prioritization framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimated hospital-onset nontuberculous-mycobacteria rates across hospitals and presented benchmark rates for hospital surveillance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hospital-network surveillance benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compared prescribing before and during quarterly benchmarking reports to evaluate their impact on outpatient antibiotic prescribing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct evaluation of whether benchmarking reports changed prescribing performance.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimated historical national antimicrobial consumption using defined daily doses to create a baseline benchmark for stewardship.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete antimicrobial-consumption benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title explicitly reports azithromycin-use results from the SHARPS-OP benchmarking collaborative.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Named concrete application within an antimicrobial-use benchmarking collaborative.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used the WHO point-prevalence survey methodology to benchmark hospital antibiotic prescribing and identify stewardship deficits.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Developed validated electronic-health-record queries and used them across two sites to benchmark guideline-consistent pediatric oncology care.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated risk-adjusted observed-to-expected ratios and compared institutional surgical-infection outcomes with NSQIP benchmarks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete surgical-infection performance benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated achievable benchmarks of care from the highest-performing clinicians across 12 emergency departments using the pared-mean method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multisite application of an established benchmarking process.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrated standardized national HAI surveillance data and compared infection rates with NHSN pooled means to establish national benchmarks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Developed new risk-standardized DOT and DASC metrics for benchmarking inpatient antibiotic use and compared them with a SAAR-like metric.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluated data suitability, case-identification validity, and anticipated usefulness of automated hospital-onset bloodstream-infection surveillance, including benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct technical and outcome-validity evaluation of an automated surveillance approach.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied capture-recapture to estimate the size of Hangzhou's sexually active homosexual male population.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hidden-population size-estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used capture-recapture counts and a Lincoln formula to estimate male sex-worker populations in three Nigerian cities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implemented capture and recapture recruitment phases to estimate the population of people who inject drugs in Washington, DC.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and applies successive-sampling population-size estimation; capture-recapture is mentioned only as a separate comparator requiring additional data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used direct capture-recapture with tagged female sex workers across capture and recapture phases to estimate population size.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducted a multistage capture-recapture exercise using unique objects and sampled venues to estimate venue-based female sex workers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hidden-population size-estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used conventional two-source capture-recapture with distributed objects to estimate MSM and female sex-worker populations in Ugandan towns.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hidden-population size-estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performed three independent captures and used two-source and Bayesian three-source capture-recapture models to estimate key populations in Kampala.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hidden-population size-estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Employed three-source capture-recapture sampling to estimate Rwanda's street- and venue-based female sex-worker population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used three-source capture-recapture and recapture histories to estimate female sex-worker population size in Hai Phong.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application, with comparison against successive-sampling estimates.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied capture-recapture and three-source capture-recapture alongside other approaches to estimate female sex-worker populations across Ghana.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete national hidden-population size-estimation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzed three-source capture-recapture data with Bayesian nonparametric latent-class models to estimate key populations in seven Nigerian states.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calculated two-source capture-recapture estimates from two respondent-driven sampling rounds for people who inject drugs in Vietnam.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied capture-recapture to estimate the population size of transgender women in Tianjin.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used three capture rounds with unique objects and Bayesian model averaging to estimate street- and venue-based female sex workers in Rwanda.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applied two- and three-source capture-recapture, compared their estimates with other methods, and examined differences in who each capture approach reached.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Treated five respondent-driven sampling rounds as capture sources and fitted log-linear capture-recapture models to estimate people who inject drugs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Used two captures to estimate both the MSM population and the number and prevalence of mpox cases among MSM in Chengdu.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Developed a mathematical within-host antibody-kinetics model intended to enable seroconversion-rate and seroincidence estimation from cross-sectional sera.
